title: lonicera fragrantissima subsp. standishii
intro: Lonicera fragrantissima subsp. standishii, also known as the Honeysuckle Bush or Fragrant Honeysuckle, is a type of flowering plant native to East Asia, characterized by its fragrant white flowers and woody stems. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can help to treat wounds, fever, and respiratory issues. The health benefits of Lonicera fragrantissima subsp. standishii include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve respiratory function, and boost the immune system, making it a popular herbal remedy for colds, flu, and other illnesses. Herbal preparations of the plant include tea, infusions, and tinctures, which can be made by drying the flowers and leaves and steeping them in hot water or a solvent.
